primary_meaning: epiphyte, a plant that grows on another plant without parasitizing it; an air plant
secondary_meaning: any organism that lives on the surface of another organism without harming it
usage_notes: Botanical term. Often: 'plante épiphyte', 'orchidée épiphyte', 'mousse épiphyte'.
common_collocations: plante épiphyte, orchidée épiphyte, écosystème épiphyte, flore épiphyte
synonyms: air plant, plante aérienne, plante commensale
image_search_query: epiphytic plant orchid air plant growing on tree branch
image_context: Colorful epiphytic orchids growing on tree branch without harming host
example_1: Les orchidées sont des plantes épiphytes qui prospèrent sur les branches d'arbres.
example_1_translation: Orchids are epiphytic plants that thrive on tree branches.
example_2: L'épiphyte ne tire pas sa nourriture de son hôte mais de l'air et de la pluie.
example_2_translation: The epiphyte does not get its food from its host but from air and rain.
example_3: Les forêts tropicales regorgent d'épiphytes créant un écosystème complexe.
example_3_translation: Tropical forests are filled with epiphytes creating a complex ecosystem.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: e-pi-FIT. Stress on final syllable.
memory_hook: Épiphyte - from Greek 'epi' (upon) + 'phyton' (plant) - plant upon plant
related_words: orchidée, air plant, écosystème, symbiose, commensalisme
common_mistakes: Not parasitaire; épiphytes don't harm their host

primary_meaning: feral, wild-tempered, unsociable; fiercely independent or resistant to change
secondary_meaning: shy or withdrawn; expressing distrust or aversion
usage_notes: Formal/literary. Agrees with noun: 'enfant farouche', 'regard farouche'. Often literary tone.
common_collocations: enfant farouche, regard farouche, tempérament farouche, allure farouche
image_search_query: wild untamed fierce fearful withdrawn shy untrusting
image_context: Representation of wild untamed nature or fierce protective animal
example_1: L'enfant farouche refusait tout contact physique avec les inconnus.
example_1_translation: The feral child refused any physical contact with strangers.
example_2: Malgré son air farouche et menaçant, elle cachait une grande tendresse.
example_2_translation: Despite her fierce and threatening demeanor, she hid great tenderness.
example_3: Ce farouche défenseur de la liberté ne cédait jamais aux pressions.
example_3_translation: This fierce defender of freedom never yielded to pressure.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: fa-ROUCH. Stress on second syllable.
memory_hook: Farouche - from Old French 'fier' (proud) + Anglo-Saxon 'farah' (wild) - wild pride

primary_meaning: geophagous, relating to the practice of eating earth, soil, or clay
secondary_meaning: describing organisms or behaviors involving consumption of mineral substances
common_collocations: pratiques géophages, comportement géophage, population géophage, habitude géophage
image_search_query: geophagia earth eating clay soil mineral consumption anthropology
image_context: Anthropological documentation of earth or clay consumption practices
example_1: Les pratiques géophages sont documentées dans plusieurs cultures traditionnelles africaines.
example_1_translation: Geophagous practices are documented in several traditional African cultures.
example_2: La géophagie désigne la consommation intentionnelle de terre ou d'argille.
example_2_translation: Geophagia refers to the intentional consumption of earth or clay.
example_3: Certaines populations géophages consomment de l'argille pour ses propriétés curatives.
example_3_translation: Certain geophagous populations consume clay for its healing properties.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: je-o-FAZHJ. Stress on final syllable.

primary_meaning: heuristic, a method of discovery or learning by trial and error; practical trial methods
secondary_meaning: relating to or serving as an aid to learning or discovery
usage_notes: Psychological/cognitive science context. Often: 'méthode heuristique', 'procédé heuristique', 'biais heuristique'.
common_collocations: méthode heuristique, procédé heuristique, biais heuristique, approche heuristique
image_search_query: discovery learning trial and error problem solving cognitive method
image_context: Illustration of learning process and trial-and-error discovery
example_1: L'heuristique de découverte permet de trouver des solutions par tâtonnement progressif.
example_1_translation: The discovery heuristic allows finding solutions through progressive trial.
example_2: Les heuristiques cognitives peuvent mener à des biais de raisonnement importants.
example_2_translation: Cognitive heuristics can lead to significant reasoning biases.
example_3: Cette approche heuristique favorise l'apprentissage autonome et créatif.
example_3_translation: This heuristic approach promotes autonomous and creative learning.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: ø-ris-TIK. Stress on final syllable. Nasal 'ø' opening.
memory_hook: Heuristique - from Greek 'heuriskein' (to find/discover) - discovery method
common_mistakes: Not anglicized 'hyoo-'; keep French nasal 'ø' sound

primary_meaning: iconography, the study of symbolic images and visual conventions in art and culture
secondary_meaning: the collective symbolism and imagery of a particular artist or period
usage_notes: Art history context. Often: 'iconographie médiévale', 'iconographie religieuse', 'analyse iconographique'.
common_collocations: iconographie médiévale, iconographie religieuse, iconographie chrétienne, analyse iconographique
synonyms: symbolique visuelle, étude d'images, imagerie, symbolisme
image_search_query: religious iconography medieval art symbols Christian imagery visual tradition
image_context: Medieval religious artwork showing complex symbolic imagery
example_1: L'iconographie médiévale est riche en symboles religieux et allégoriques.
example_1_translation: Medieval iconography is rich in religious and allegorical symbols.
example_2: L'iconographie de ce peintre renvoie constamment à la mythologie antique.
example_2_translation: This painter's iconography constantly refers to ancient mythology.
example_3: L'étude iconographique révèle les significations cachées du tableau.
example_3_translation: Iconographic analysis reveals the hidden meanings of the painting.
pronunciation_tips: Five syllables: i-ko-no-GRA-fi. Stress on fourth syllable.
memory_hook: Iconographie - from Greek 'eikon' (image) + 'graphia' (study/writing) - study of images
related_words: symbole, signification, art, traditions visuelles, allégorie

primary_meaning: juxtaposition, the act of placing two things side by side for contrasting effect
secondary_meaning: a literary or artistic technique using proximity to highlight contrasts
usage_notes: Literary/rhetorical context. Often: 'juxtaposition de', 'effet de juxtaposition'.
common_collocations: juxtaposition de, juxtaposition avec, effet de juxtaposition, juxtaposition saisissante
synonyms: mise côte à côte, contraste, opposition, proximité
image_search_query: contrast juxtaposition opposing elements side by side comparison
image_context: Artistic or literary example of contrasting elements placed adjacently
example_1: La juxtaposition de ces deux concepts révèle leur opposition fondamentale.
example_1_translation: The juxtaposition of these two concepts reveals their fundamental opposition.
example_2: L'artiste utilise la juxtaposition pour créer un contraste saisissant et provocateur.
example_2_translation: The artist uses juxtaposition to create striking and provocative contrast.
example_3: Cette juxtaposition d'images renforce le message politique de l'œuvre.
example_3_translation: This juxtaposition of images reinforces the political message of the work.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: jyk-sta-po-ZI-sion. Stress on third syllable. Nasal 'on'.
related_words: contraste, opposition, proximité, côte à côte, effet

primary_meaning: kinesthetic, relating to the sensation of body movement and position in space
secondary_meaning: learning or teaching that emphasizes physical movement and sensory experience
common_collocations: apprentissage kinesthésique, sens kinesthésique, expérience kinesthésique, perception kinesthésique
image_search_query: kinesthetic learning body movement proprioception spatial awareness sensation
image_context: Illustration of body movement and spatial proprioception
example_1: L'apprentissage kinesthésique convient particulièrement aux élèves qui apprennent par le mouvement.
example_1_translation: Kinesthetic learning suits students who learn through physical movement.
example_2: Les récepteurs kinesthésiques captent les variations de position et de tension musculaire.
example_2_translation: Kinesthetic receptors detect variations in body position and muscle tension.
example_3: Cette approche kinesthésique renforce la mémorisation par l'expérience incarnée.
example_3_translation: This kinesthetic approach strengthens memorization through embodied experience.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: ki-nes-the-ZIK. Stress on final syllable.

primary_meaning: logomachy, a dispute or argument about words; a verbal fight over definitions
secondary_meaning: futile dispute more concerned with terminology than substance
common_collocations: logomachie stérile, pure logomachie, logomachie verbale, sombrer en logomachie
synonyms: dispute de mots, querelle terminologique, chicane verbale
antonyms: débat constructif, dialogue fructueux, argumentation substantielle
image_search_query: word dispute debate argument fighting over words futile semantics
image_context: Illustration of verbal argument or debate over words
example_1: La logomachie entre les deux philosophes portait sur les définitions précises.
example_1_translation: The logomachy between the two philosophers concerned precise definitions.
example_2: Cette dispute n'était qu'une logomachie stérile sans intérêt pratique.
example_2_translation: This dispute was only a sterile logomachy without practical interest.
example_3: Ils se sont perdus dans une logomachie interminable sur le sens exact du mot.
example_3_translation: They got lost in an endless logomachy over the exact meaning of the word.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: lo-go-MA-chi. Stress on third syllable.
common_mistakes: Not meaningless argument but specifically about words/terminology

primary_meaning: mnesic, relating to memory or the retention of knowledge
secondary_meaning: concerning memory disorders or memory function
common_collocations: troubles mnésiques, fonction mnésique, capacité mnésique, déficit mnésique
image_search_query: memory brain cognition remembering mental retention recall
image_context: Brain illustration showing memory centers and neural pathways
example_1: Les troubles mnésiques sont fréquents chez les personnes atteintes d'Alzheimer.
example_1_translation: Mnesic disorders are common in people with Alzheimer's disease.
example_2: La fonction mnésique peut être altérée par divers facteurs psychologiques et physiologiques.
example_2_translation: Mnesic function can be altered by various psychological and physiological factors.
example_3: L'évaluation mnésique révéla des déficits spécifiques de mémoire à court terme.
example_3_translation: Mnesic evaluation revealed specific short-term memory deficits.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: mne-ZIK. Stress on final syllable. Silent 'm' at start.

primary_meaning: noological, relating to the intellect or pure thought; concerned with the realm of ideas
secondary_meaning: of or pertaining to noology, the science of the mind
usage_notes: Very formal/philosophical context. Rare usage. Often: 'domaine noologique', 'niveau noologique'.
common_collocations: domaine noologique, niveau noologique, réalité noologique, analyse noologique
image_search_query: intellect philosophy pure thought abstract mind ideas concepts
image_context: Abstract philosophical visualization of pure intellectual realm
example_1: La distinction noologique entre différents types de cognition est fondamentale.
example_1_translation: The noological distinction between different types of cognition is fundamental.
example_2: Le domaine noologique examine les structures de la pensée pure.
example_2_translation: The noological domain examines the structures of pure thought.
example_3: Cette analyse noologique révèle les catégories mentales sous-jacentes.
example_3_translation: This noological analysis reveals underlying mental categories.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: no-o-lo-JIK. Stress on final syllable.
memory_hook: Noologique - from Greek 'noos' (mind/intellect) + '-logie' (study) - study of mind
common_mistakes: Very rare; avoid unless in serious philosophical context

primary_meaning: ontological, relating to being, existence, or the nature of reality itself
secondary_meaning: in logic, the ontological argument pertaining to proof of God's existence
usage_notes: Philosophical context. Often: 'argument ontologique', 'question ontologique', 'statut ontologique'.
common_collocations: argument ontologique, question ontologique, statut ontologique, preuve ontologique
image_search_query: being existence philosophy ontology abstract reality abstract concept
image_context: Philosophical illustration symbolizing the nature of being and existence
example_1: L'argument ontologique vise à prouver l'existence de Dieu par la raison pure.
example_1_translation: The ontological argument aims to prove God's existence through pure reason.
example_2: La question ontologique fondamentale porte sur le sens et la nature de l'être.
example_2_translation: The fundamental ontological question concerns the meaning and nature of being.
example_3: Le statut ontologique de ce concept reste débattu par les philosophes.
example_3_translation: The ontological status of this concept remains debated by philosophers.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: on-to-lo-JIK. Stress on final syllable. Nasal 'on'.
memory_hook: Ontologique - from Greek 'on' (being) + '-logie' (study) - study of being

primary_meaning: phytosome, a plant-derived active compound complexed with phospholipids for enhanced absorption
secondary_meaning: a liposomal complex of plant extracts used in herbal medicine
usage_notes: Biochemistry/supplement context. Often: 'phytosome de curcuma', 'phytosome de silymarine'.
common_collocations: phytosome de, phytosome de curcuma, biodisponibilité phytosome, absorption phytosome
synonyms: complexe phytolipide, préparation de plante, extrait amélioré
image_search_query: phytosome turmeric plant supplement herbal complex molecular structure
image_context: Scientific illustration of phytosome molecular structure
example_1: Le phytosome de curcuma possède une biodisponibilité significativement accrue.
example_1_translation: Turmeric phytosome has significantly increased bioavailability.
example_2: Les phytosomes sont utilisés pour améliorer l'absorption des principes actifs.
example_2_translation: Phytosomes are used to improve absorption of active compounds.
example_3: Cette formulation phytosome renforce l'efficacité thérapeutique du silymarin.
example_3_translation: This phytosome formulation enhances the therapeutic efficacy of silymarin.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: fi-to-SOM. Stress on final syllable.

secondary_meaning: consisting of five parts or elements
usage_notes: Adjective. Modifies nouns: 'production quintuple', 'effet quintuple'. Can be used as noun (le quintuple).
common_collocations: production quintuple, augmentation quintuple, effet quintuple, le quintuple de
synonyms: cinq fois plus, multiplié par cinq, fivefold
image_search_query: fivefold multiplication five times increase multiplication factor
image_context: Visual representation of five-fold increase or multiplication
example_1: La production quintuple de cette année dépasse toutes les projections précédentes.
example_1_translation: This year's fivefold production exceeds all previous projections.
example_2: Le développement quintuple de la technologie a transformé complètement la société.
example_2_translation: The fivefold development of technology completely transformed society.
example_3: Cette augmentation quintuple représente une croissance exceptionnelle.
example_3_translation: This fivefold increase represents exceptional growth.
pronunciation_tips: Two syllables: kin-TU-pl. Nasal 'in'. Stress on second syllable.

primary_meaning: reversible, capable of reversing or being reversed; proceeding in opposite directions
secondary_meaning: in thermodynamics, a process that can theoretically return to its initial state
common_collocations: réaction réversible, processus réversible, équilibre réversible, transformation réversible
synonyms: réversoire, qui peut être inversé, bidirectionnel, réversif
image_search_query: reversible reaction chemistry equilibrium reversible process arrows
image_context: Chemistry diagram showing reversible reaction with arrows in both directions
example_1: La réaction chimique réversible peut procéder dans les deux sens selon les conditions.
example_1_translation: The reversible chemical reaction can proceed in both directions per conditions.
example_2: Le processus n'est pas réversible une fois que la transformation est achevée.
example_2_translation: The process is not reversible once the transformation is complete.
example_3: Cette veste réversible peut être portée des deux côtés.
example_3_translation: This reversible jacket can be worn from either side.
pronunciation_tips: Four syllables: re-ver-SI-bl. Stress on third syllable.
common_mistakes: Not just 'can be turned around'; physical science term

primary_meaning: stochastic, involving randomness or probability; determined by chance
secondary_meaning: in mathematics, relating to random processes with definable probability distributions
common_collocations: modèle stochastique, processus stochastique, variable stochastique, simulation stochastique
image_search_query: random probability distribution stochastic process uncertainty modeling
image_context: Visualization of random distribution and probability patterns
example_1: Le modèle stochastique intègre l'incertitude dans les prédictions financières.
example_1_translation: The stochastic model incorporates uncertainty into financial predictions.
example_2: La processus stochastique est utilisé pour modéliser les marchés financiers.
example_2_translation: The stochastic process is used to model financial markets.
example_3: Cette simulation stochastique révèle les variations possibles du système.
example_3_translation: This stochastic simulation reveals possible variations in the system.
pronunciation_tips: Three syllables: sto-kas-TIK. Stress on final syllable.
memory_hook: Stochastique - from Greek 'stochastikos' (conjectural, aimed at) - relating to chance
